title: Pipeline Bug or Bugs2FIX item lacks sinPattern propagation when matched against a SIN-PATTERN registry entry
description: When `Invoke-SinRegistryFeedback` matches a Bug item against a SIN-PATTERN-*.json registry entry, it MUST stamp the canonical pattern id onto the item's `sinPattern` field. When `ConvertTo-Bugs2FIX` derives a FIX item from a Bug, it MUST inherit BOTH `sinId` and `sinPattern` from the parent so downstream tooling (SIN-Scoreboard.xhtml, integrity-check audits, reuse-count tracking on fixes) can reconstruct lineage. Items emitted without `sinPattern` (when one is known) are referentially orphaned and prevent fix reuse counters from incrementing on the originating SIN-PATTERN.
detection_method: Scan todo/Bug-*.json + todo/Bugs2FIX-*.json: items whose `notes` matches /Matched existing sin: SIN-PATTERN-/ MUST have non-empty `sinPattern`. Bugs2FIX items with non-empty parentId MUST inherit both sinId and sinPattern from the matching parent.
remediation: Patched in `modules/CronAiAthon-Pipeline.psm1`: New-PipelineItem now accepts -SinPattern; Invoke-SinRegistryFeedback stamps it on PATTERN-file matches; ConvertTo-Bugs2FIX propagates parent's sinId AND sinPattern. Backfill historical orphans via scripts/Sync-PipelineRegistryFromDisk.ps1 -Apply.
examples:
# Bad - Bug matched a SIN-PATTERN but lost the linkage
{ "id": "Bug-...", "sinId": "SIN-PATTERN-027", "sinPattern": "", "notes": "Matched existing sin: ..." }
